title: microcarpaea cochlearifolia
intro: Microcarpaea cochlearifolia, also known as the shellflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Haloragaceae. This plant is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Polynesian medicine. The medicinal properties of Microcarpaea cochlearifolia include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Microcarpaea cochlearifolia is typically prepared as a herbal tea or decoction, and it is also used in traditional remedies for its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties.
